title: Comments in included files break build
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**
I tried to include one Dockerfile into another one with the help of the preprocessor feature. That fails if there is a comment in the second file. First file has ending `.in`, second file doesn't.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Make two Dockerfiles, one with file ending .in, the other one without. `#include` the second file in the first.

```
Dockerfile.in:
#include "./Dockerfile"
FROM ubuntu:latest

Dockerfile:
#just a comment
```
2. `podman build -f Dockerfile.in .`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: error pre-processing Dockerfile: In file included from <stdin>:2:
./Dockerfile:1: error: invalid preprocessing directive #just
    1 | # just a comment
      |: exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
No error :)
From the (limited) documentation of the command I was under the impression that only files ending with `.in` will be preprocessed. Apparently also the included file gets  preprocessed which gives an error as there is a comment. Is this intended behavior? If so, it seems like a big limitation not to be able to use comments. Is there a workaround?

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.1.2
cpp version 10.2.0
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body: Going to transfer this over to Buildah.

Apparently I don't have permission to do that...

@TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind PTAL

> #just a comment

That is a comment for Dockerfiles but not for the CPP (where it should adhere to C comments `//` or `/*`).  I do not have a solution for that other than not using Dockerfile-style comments.  I am not sure whether we can instruct `cpp` to only replace `#include`s but not evaluate other macros.

We kind of want
``` 
# ifdef ...
#endif 
```
To work also.

It would be nice if we could tell cpp to ignore errors.

I wonder how hard it would be to write a preprocessor and not use cpp.

> We kind of want
> 
> ```
> # ifdef ...
> #endif 
> ```
> 
> To work also.

^ that works already.

> It would be nice if we could tell cpp to ignore errors.

Interesting idea!  Yes, let's see if we can massage cpp to eat a Dockerfile-style comment.

> I wonder how hard it would be to write a preprocessor and not use cpp.

I hope we don't have to :)
